Video game theme

This article is about the use of sexual acts and nudity in video games. For depiction of sexual orientation or gender issues, see [LGBTQ themes in video games](/wiki/LGBTQ_themes_in_video_games "LGBTQ themes in video games").

"Erotic game" redirects here. For the video game genre, see [Eroge](/wiki/Eroge "Eroge").

[Sexual content](/wiki/Sexual_content "Sexual content") has featured in [video games](/wiki/Video_game "Video game") since the early days of the [industry](/wiki/Video_game_industry "Video game industry"), and games featuring sexual content can be found on most [platforms](/wiki/Computing_platform "Computing platform") and can be of any [video game genre](/wiki/Video_game_genre "Video game genre").[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

The inclusion of sex in games has been subject to varying levels of controversy over the decades, sometimes resulting in calls for increased [regulation](/wiki/Video_game_content_rating_system "Video game content rating system") and legislation dealing directly with adult content.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In Western gaming, the promise of sexual content in games is commonly used as a marketing tool, but many highly sexualized games do not feature any explicitly adult content.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Though some games do use [sex acts](/wiki/Human_sexual_activity "Human sexual activity") or [nudity](/wiki/Nudity "Nudity") as a [narrative device](/wiki/Plot_device "Plot device"), in-game reward, or a gameplay element, purely pornographic games are uncommon.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

However, the [Japanese](/wiki/Japan "Japan") pornographic [eroge](/wiki/Eroge "Eroge") subgenre is popular worldwide. First appearing in the 1980s, these games vary significantly in narrative complexity as well as the level of [interactivity](/wiki/Interactivity "Interactivity"), taking forms ranging from the [visual novel](/wiki/Visual_novel "Visual novel") to [virtual reality](/wiki/Virtual_reality "Virtual reality") experiences.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sexual_content_in_video_games&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### 1980s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sexual_content_in_video_games&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: 1980s")]

One of the earliest video games to feature sexual themes was the 1981 [text-based](/wiki/Interactive_fiction "Interactive fiction") *[Softporn Adventure](/wiki/Softporn_Adventure "Softporn Adventure")*, published by [On-Line Systems](/wiki/Sierra_Entertainment "Sierra Entertainment") for the [Apple II](/wiki/Apple_II "Apple II"). Despite heavy piracy, the game still sold 25,000 copies, roughly equivalent to 25% of the number of Apple II computers sold at the time. In a 1981 article in *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")*, On-Line reported that they were making a version of the game for straight women, though this never materialized.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Several pornographic games, such as *[X-Man](/wiki/X-Man_(video_game) "X-Man (video game)"),* were created for the [Atari 2600](/wiki/Atari_2600 "Atari 2600") in the early 1980s. The video game company, [American Multiple Industries](/wiki/American_Multiple_Industries "American Multiple Industries"), released three unlicensed games for the Atari 2600; *[Beat 'Em & Eat 'Em](/wiki/Beat_%27Em_%26_Eat_%27Em "Beat 'Em & Eat 'Em")*, *[Custer's Revenge](/wiki/Custer%27s_Revenge "Custer's Revenge")*, and *[Bachelor Party](/wiki/Bachelor_Party_(video_game) "Bachelor Party (video game)")*.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) The games were poorly received (particularly *Custer's Revenge,* which is considered to be one of the [worst games ever made](/wiki/List_of_video_games_notable_for_negative_reception "List of video games notable for negative reception")), and AMI [went out of business in 1983](/wiki/Video_game_crash_of_1983 "Video game crash of 1983").[[8]](#cite_note-8) Company [PlayAround](/wiki/PlayAround "PlayAround") purchased the rights to these games and began distributing them under new titles in 2-in-1 cartridges.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In 1983, Entertainment Enterprises, Ltd. released an arcade game called *Swinging Singles,* which was mostly meant for [bars](/wiki/Bar_(establishment) "Bar (establishment)"), [adult stores](/wiki/Sex_shop "Sex shop"), and [sex clubs](/wiki/Sex_club "Sex club"). The game requires the player to drive through the city maze, gathering dots like *[Pac-Man](/wiki/Pac-Man "Pac-Man")* before reaching a [brothel](/wiki/Brothel "Brothel") where the player is required to fight off [venereal disease](/wiki/Sexually_transmitted_infection "Sexually transmitted infection") and collect keys to unlock sex scenes.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Also released in 1983 was *Strip Poker: A Sizzling Game of Chance* for the [Commodore 64](/wiki/Commodore_64 "Commodore 64") and other [8-bit platforms](/wiki/Third_generation_of_video_game_consoles "Third generation of video game consoles"), which [Artworx](/wiki/Artworx "Artworx") Software created.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In 1986, [Martech](/wiki/Martech "Martech") released a [strip poker](/wiki/Strip_game#Poker "Strip game") game featuring digitized pictures of [Samantha Fox](/wiki/Samantha_Fox "Samantha Fox") on the C64, [ZX Spectrum](/wiki/ZX_Spectrum "ZX Spectrum"), and [Amstrad](/wiki/Amstrad "Amstrad"). The same company would later release the controversial *[Vixen](/wiki/Vixen_(video_game) "Vixen (video game)")*, featuring another page 3 model [Corinne Russell](/wiki/Corinne_Russell "Corinne Russell").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Also, in 1986 On-line Systems (now called [Sierra On-Line](/wiki/Sierra_Entertainment "Sierra Entertainment")) asked [game designer](/wiki/Game_design "Game design") [Al Lowe](/wiki/Al_Lowe "Al Lowe") to create an adult game in the [graphical adventure](/wiki/Adventure_game#Graphic_adventure "Adventure game") style made popular by their *[King's Quest](/wiki/King%27s_Quest "King's Quest")* series. Lowe took the basic elements of *Softporn Adventure*, greatly expanded it, and released it as the 1987 game *[Leisure Suit Larry in the Land of the Lounge Lizards](/wiki/Leisure_Suit_Larry_in_the_Land_of_the_Lounge_Lizards "Leisure Suit Larry in the Land of the Lounge Lizards")*. Sierra did not advertise the game, and retailers were loath to carry it, so initial sales were low. However, [word-of-mouth](/wiki/Word_of_mouth "Word of mouth") spread, and soon the *[Leisure Suit Larry](/wiki/Leisure_Suit_Larry "Leisure Suit Larry")* series was a success, spawning two sequels before the end of the decade, 1988's *[Leisure Suit Larry Goes Looking for Love (in Several Wrong Places)](/wiki/Leisure_Suit_Larry_Goes_Looking_for_Love_(in_Several_Wrong_Places) "Leisure Suit Larry Goes Looking for Love (in Several Wrong Places)")*, and 1989's *[Leisure Suit Larry III: Passionate Patti in Pursuit of the Pulsating Pectorals](/wiki/Leisure_Suit_Larry_III:_Passionate_Patti_in_Pursuit_of_the_Pulsating_Pectorals "Leisure Suit Larry III: Passionate Patti in Pursuit of the Pulsating Pectorals")*.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### 1990s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sexual_content_in_video_games&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 1990s")]

With [CD-ROM](/wiki/CD-ROM "CD-ROM") and multimedia-based games in the 1990s, most adult games featured video clips with limited interactivity. Both pre-rendered and real-time [3D graphics](/wiki/3D_computer_graphics "3D computer graphics") were also used. While most games could be considered nothing more than [pornography](/wiki/Pornography "Pornography"), some attempted to include actual stories and plots. This can be seen in some games with less explicit content, equal to an [R](/wiki/Motion_Picture_Association_film_rating_system#R "Motion Picture Association film rating system")-rated or [PG-13](/wiki/Motion_Picture_Association_film_rating_system#PG-13 "Motion Picture Association film rating system")-rated movie.

1990 saw the release of the first game in the *[Gals Panic](/wiki/Kaneko#List_of_games "Kaneko")* series. The game derived gameplay elements from an older title called *[Qix](/wiki/Qix "Qix")*, which did not have sexual images. The object of the games is to slowly remove pieces of the playing field while avoiding an enemy or groups of enemies. Removing the playing field slowly reveals pictures of [models](/wiki/Model_(person) "Model (person)") in escalating states of undress. The game had nine sequels throughout the 1990s (with a final game in 2002).

*[Voyeur](/wiki/Voyeur_(video_game) "Voyeur (video game)")* by Philips came out in 1993 for their [CD-i](/wiki/CD-i "CD-i") console. While the game does not show any explicit nudity, it allows the player to spy on several characters in [lingerie](/wiki/Lingerie "Lingerie"), and [foreplay](/wiki/Foreplay "Foreplay") and explores taboos such as [BDSM](/wiki/BDSM "BDSM") and [incest](/wiki/Incest "Incest").

In the 1990s, [NEC](/wiki/NEC "NEC") (on the [PC Engine](/wiki/TurboGrafx-16 "TurboGrafx-16") series and [PC-FX](/wiki/PC-FX "PC-FX")) and [Sega](/wiki/Sega "Sega") (on [Sega Saturn](/wiki/Sega_Saturn "Sega Saturn")) were the only companies who officially allowed sexual content on their consoles in Japan[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*], but eroge was more prevalent on the NEC [PC-98](/wiki/PC-98 "PC-98") and [FM Towns](/wiki/FM_Towns "FM Towns") computer platforms.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### 2000s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sexual_content_in_video_games&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2000s")]

|  |  |

Modern console publishers often have policies against depictions of nudity and explicit sexuality, particularly [Sony Computer Entertainment](/wiki/Sony_Interactive_Entertainment "Sony Interactive Entertainment") with its [PlayStation](/wiki/PlayStation "PlayStation") brand of consoles. *[BMX XXX](/wiki/BMX_XXX "BMX XXX")* for the [PlayStation 2](/wiki/PlayStation_2 "PlayStation 2") was censored in the American release, while versions on the [Xbox](/wiki/Xbox_(console) "Xbox (console)") and [GameCube](/wiki/GameCube "GameCube") were not and featured nudity.[[16]](#cite_note-16) However, Sony allowed nudity in the title *[God of War](/wiki/God_of_War_(2005_video_game) "God of War (2005 video game)")* (2005), and also developed by Sony, a game based on [Greek mythology](/wiki/Greek_mythology "Greek mythology") set in [Ancient Greece](/wiki/Ancient_Greece "Ancient Greece"), where nudity was perceived differently. Nudity was subsequently featured in the game's sequels, *[God of War II](/wiki/God_of_War_II "God of War II")* (2007), *[God of War: Chains of Olympus](/wiki/God_of_War:_Chains_of_Olympus "God of War: Chains of Olympus")* (2008), *[God of War III](/wiki/God_of_War_III "God of War III")* (2010), *[God of War: Ghost of Sparta](/wiki/God_of_War:_Ghost_of_Sparta "God of War: Ghost of Sparta")* (2010), and *[God of War: Ascension](/wiki/God_of_War:_Ascension "God of War: Ascension")* (2013), which were also based on Greek mythology. The [full-motion video](/wiki/Full-motion_video "Full-motion video") quiz game *[The Guy Game](/wiki/The_Guy_Game "The Guy Game")* (2004) gained notoriety for its use of an underage model. The scenes in question led to a lawsuit by a woman who explained that she was only seventeen years old when the footage was taken, thus classifying it as [child pornography](/wiki/Child_pornography "Child pornography"), which is illegal to own or sell in some countries.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Sex scenes and nudity have also appeared in [Quantic Dream](/wiki/Quantic_Dream "Quantic Dream")'s *[Fahrenheit](/wiki/Fahrenheit_(2005_video_game) "Fahrenheit (2005 video game)")* and *[Heavy Rain](/wiki/Heavy_Rain "Heavy Rain")*, released for the PlayStation 2 and [PlayStation 3](/wiki/PlayStation_3 "PlayStation 3") respectively.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

Sexuality and sexual frustration is also a theme prevalent in the *[Silent Hill](/wiki/Silent_Hill "Silent Hill")* series of survival-horror games.

A new generation of adult social games has emerged that bring multiple users together in sexual environments. Examples include *[Red Light Center](/wiki/Red_Light_Center "Red Light Center"), [Singles: Flirt Up Your Life](/wiki/Singles:_Flirt_Up_Your_Life "Singles: Flirt Up Your Life")* and *[Playboy: The Mansion](/wiki/Playboy:_The_Mansion "Playboy: The Mansion")*. While it is not explicitly intended for purely adult-oriented entertainment, the virtual world of *[Second Life](/wiki/Second_Life "Second Life")*, which is made up almost entirely of player-made content, has an array of very exotic adult entertainment including nudity and full-on sexual activities.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Adult games may take the form of [bootlegs](/wiki/Copyright_infringement "Copyright infringement"), circumventing mainstream publishers who may have policies against such games. [Patches](/wiki/Patch_(computing) "Patch (computing)") or [hacks](/wiki/Hacker "Hacker") to mainstream non-adult games may add sexual and pornographic themes, mostly for [humor](/wiki/Humour "Humour"), especially when sexuality was never intended in the original game. Examples include the *[Tomb Raider](/wiki/Tomb_Raider "Tomb Raider")* video games, the *[Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas](/wiki/Grand_Theft_Auto:_San_Andreas "Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas")* [Hot Coffee mod](/wiki/Hot_Coffee_(minigame) "Hot Coffee (minigame)"), *[The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion](/wiki/The_Elder_Scrolls_IV:_Oblivion "The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion")*, *[The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim](/wiki/The_Elder_Scrolls_V:_Skyrim "The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim")*, and the *[Half-Life 2](/wiki/Half-Life_2 "Half-Life 2")* FakeFactory Cinematic [mod](/wiki/Video_game_modding "Video game modding") and [ROM](/wiki/Read-only_memory "Read-only memory") hacks for console [emulators](/wiki/Emulator "Emulator").

The Internet has allowed adult games to receive wider availability and recognition, including amateur games in [Adobe Flash](/wiki/Adobe_Flash "Adobe Flash") or Java. It has also allowed amateurs to create and distribute adult text adventure games, known as "Adult Interactive Fiction" or AIF.[[20]](#cite_note-Apophis-20)

Modern consumer [virtual reality headsets](/wiki/Virtual_reality_headset "Virtual reality headset"), such as the [Oculus Rift](/wiki/Oculus_Rift "Oculus Rift") and [HTC Vive](/wiki/HTC_Vive "HTC Vive"), allow users to engage in [virtual sex](/wiki/Virtual_sex "Virtual sex") through simulated environments. An example is *[VR Kanojo](/wiki/VR_Kanojo "VR Kanojo")*.

### 2010s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sexual_content_in_video_games&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2010s")]

[Blizzard Entertainment](/wiki/Blizzard_Entertainment "Blizzard Entertainment")'s 2016 game *[Overwatch](/wiki/Overwatch_(video_game) "Overwatch (video game)")* has inspired a notable amount of [fan-made porn](/wiki/Overwatch_and_pornography "Overwatch and pornography"). Short porn videos featuring official characters have been animated via [Source Filmmaker](/wiki/Source_Filmmaker "Source Filmmaker") and are popular on porn sites such as [Pornhub](/wiki/Pornhub "Pornhub"). Despite its popularity, Blizzard has issued [cease-and-desist](/wiki/Cease_and_desist "Cease and desist") orders to some of the creators.

The Witcher 3 is a prominent mainstream game featuring several sex scenes and nudity from both romantic encounters and visits to brothels, some of which is important to the plot of the story.

[Indie games](/wiki/Indie_game "Indie game") have expanded the reach of adult games, and [Steam](/wiki/Steam_(service) "Steam (service)") now allows adult games onto its storefront.[[21]](#cite_note-21) [Patreon](/wiki/Patreon "Patreon") provides direct funding to adult developers, allowing games like *[Summertime Saga](/wiki/Summertime_Saga "Summertime Saga")* and *[Hardcoded](/wiki/Hardcoded_(video_game) "Hardcoded (video game)")* to appeal to niche markets that previously would not have been created.[[22]](#cite_note-byte-22)[[23]](#cite_note-Kotaku2018-23)

The continuing rise of [open-source software](/wiki/Open-source_software "Open-source software") development practices has also influenced adult video games, allowing communities of amateurs to collaboratively create and distribute adult games via the Internet.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Eroge

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sexual_content_in_video_games&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Eroge")]

Main article: [Eroge](/wiki/Eroge "Eroge")

Eroge is a Japanese style adult erotic game. In 1982, Japan's [Koei](/wiki/Koei "Koei"), founded by husband-and-wife team [Yoichi](/wiki/Yoichi_Erikawa "Yoichi Erikawa") and [Keiko Erikawa](/wiki/Keiko_Erikawa "Keiko Erikawa") (and later known for [strategy video games](/wiki/Strategy_video_game "Strategy video game")), released the first erotic computer game with sexually explicit graphics, *[Night Life](/wiki/Night_Life_(video_game) "Night Life (video game)")*,[[26]](#cite_note-Retro-26) an early [graphic adventure game](/wiki/Graphic_adventure_game "Graphic adventure game")[[27]](#cite_note-27) for the [NEC PC-8801](/wiki/PC-8800_series "PC-8800 series"). That same year, Koei released another erotic title, *Danchi Tsuma no Yuwaku* (*Seduction of the Condominium Wife*),[[28]](#cite_note-Pesimo-28) which was an early [role-playing](/wiki/Role-playing_video_game "Role-playing video game")[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30) [adventure game](/wiki/Adventure_game "Adventure game") with colour graphics, owing to the eight-color palette of the [NEC PC-8001](/wiki/PC-8000_series "PC-8000 series") computer. It became a hit, helping Koei become a major software company.[[28]](#cite_note-Pesimo-28)

In another opinion, [Yuji Horii](/wiki/Yuji_Horii "Yuji Horii") recalled in 1986 that he saw a demonstration of a [Yakyūken](/wiki/Yaky%C5%ABken "Yakyūken")-like game running on the [FM-8](/wiki/FM-8 "FM-8") in the end of [1981](/wiki/1981_in_video_games "1981 in video games"), and he considered Yakyūken was the origin of adult games.[[31]](#cite_note-31) Some writers say that *[Yakyūken](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E9%87%8E%E7%90%83%E6%8B%B3_(%E3%83%8F%E3%83%89%E3%82%BD%E3%83%B3%E3%81%AE%E3%82%B2%E3%83%BC%E3%83%A0) "ja:野球拳 (ハドソンのゲーム)")* (1981) produced for [Sharp MZ](/wiki/Sharp_MZ "Sharp MZ") computers by [Hudson Soft](/wiki/Hudson_Soft "Hudson Soft") is the first Japanese adult game.[[32]](#cite_note-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33)

Like with Koei, several other now-famous Japanese companies such as [Enix](/wiki/Enix "Enix"), [Square](/wiki/Square_(video_game_company) "Square (video game company)") and [Nihon Falcom](/wiki/Nihon_Falcom "Nihon Falcom") also released erotic adult games for the [PC-8801](/wiki/PC-8800_series "PC-8800 series") computer in the early 1980s before they became mainstream. Early eroge usually had simplistic stories and extreme sexual content, such as [rape](/wiki/Rape "Rape") and [lolicon](/wiki/Lolicon "Lolicon").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] In some of the early erotic games, the erotic content is meaningfully integrated into a thoughtful and mature storyline, though others often used it as simply an excuse for pornography.[[26]](#cite_note-Retro-26)

In 1999, Key released *[Kanon](/wiki/Kanon_(visual_novel) "Kanon (visual novel)")*. It contains about 7 brief erotic scenes in a sentimental story the size of a long novel (an all-ages version was also released afterward). *Kanon* sold over 300,000 copies.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

In response to increasing pressure from Japanese [lobby groups](/wiki/Lobby_group "Lobby group"), in mid-1996 [Sega of Japan](/wiki/Sega "Sega") announced that they would no longer permit [Sega Saturn](/wiki/Sega_Saturn "Sega Saturn") games to include nudity.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

Many [fanservice](/wiki/Fanservice "Fanservice") video games with sexual content have some brief nudity. The "M" rated series *[Senran Kagura](/wiki/Senran_Kagura "Senran Kagura")* has some nudity in both gameplay and cutscenes but genitals are not displayed. Fanservice games rated "T" such as the *[Hyperdimension Neptunia](/wiki/Hyperdimension_Neptunia "Hyperdimension Neptunia")* series offer similar content but less than what *Senran Kagura* has. However, since 2018, Sony has now issued new regulations for [PlayStation 4](/wiki/PlayStation_4 "PlayStation 4") games with both fanservice and sexual content. For the localized release of *[Senran Kagura Burst Re:Newal](/wiki/Senran_Kagura_Burst_Re:Newal "Senran Kagura Burst Re:Newal")*, "Intimacy Mode", a mode where the player can play with the characters' bodies was removed, but by comparison, this mode is still available in the series' previous titles *[Senran Kagura: Estival Versus](/wiki/Senran_Kagura:_Estival_Versus "Senran Kagura: Estival Versus")* and *[Senran Kagura: Peach Beach Splash](/wiki/Senran_Kagura:_Peach_Beach_Splash "Senran Kagura: Peach Beach Splash")*. The PC release of *Burst Re:Newal* was not changed.[[36]](#cite_note-36) Many other recent PS4 games with sexual content are facing the same problem from Sony, including *Neptunia*, *[Date a Live: Rio Reincarnation](/wiki/Date_a_Live "Date a Live")*, *[Death end re;Quest](/wiki/Death_end_re;Quest "Death end re;Quest")* and the *[Nekopara](/wiki/Nekopara "Nekopara")* series, although Sony did allow nudity and a sex scene in *[The Last of Us Part II](/wiki/The_Last_of_Us_Part_II "The Last of Us Part II")*. This controversy had become a problem for the development of *[Senran Kagura 7even](/wiki/Senran_Kagura_7EVEN "Senran Kagura 7EVEN")*, in which publishers [Marvelous](/wiki/Marvelous_(company) "Marvelous (company)") are now reconsidering the game[[37]](#cite_note-37) and series producer Kenichiro Takaki is now leaving Marvelous.[[38]](#cite_note-38)
